title: Building VFAM - Quantifying Advice Value
content: You know what kept me up at night for years? [pause] Investors would ask 'What am I getting for these fees?' and we didn't have a good answer. We'd say things like 'peace of mind' or 'behavioral coaching' - all true, but vague. [pause] So we built VFAM - the Vanguard Financial Advice Model. It lets us actually quantify the value of specific advice for specific people. [pause] Not perfectly - you can't put a number on everything - but much better than 'trust me, it's worth it.' [laughter] The research changed how I think about advice. It's not about beating a benchmark. It's about: will this person reach their goals? Will they avoid the catastrophic outcomes? Will they be okay?
follow_up: The case studies are my favorite part. Four different people, four completely different answers to 'how can advice help?' One person needed to save more. Another needed to spend more. Same framework, completely different conclusions. That's the power of personalization.
lesson: The value of advice isn't a fixed number - it depends entirely on your unique situation, goals, and circumstances.
